action.hypothesis: `two_stage_residual` has now produced a real full-fidelity gain at K=128, so the next informative move is to map whether that architectural win also strengthens the lower-K Pareto frontier rather than assuming the gain is confined to the anchor point. Running the same strong recipe at K=64 isolates whether staged residual allocation preserves enough of the new quality advantage to create a better non-dominated tradeoff than the current gated K=64 point.
action.summary: Run a `two_stage_residual` proxy comparison at K=64 using the same signum/EF=16/aux-free recipe that just improved the K=128 full frontier, changing only K.
action.self_review: This keeps the search on the newly validated winning family and changes only K, which is the cleanest way to test whether the new architecture improves the broader Pareto frontier rather than only the K=128 anchor. Risk: the staged residual design may rely on higher budget and degrade sharply at K=64, but that is exactly the missing frontier information.
notes_to_memory: Round 7 established `two_stage_residual` as the new K=128 full frontier leader with FVU 0.13607123959809542, materially beating gated.
This round tests whether the same architecture also improves the lower-K Pareto frontier by changing only K from 128 to 64 under the winning recipe.
next_hypotheses: If `two_stage_residual` at K=64 beats the current gated K=64 frontier point, keep extending the family down the K axis, including K=32.
If K=64 regresses sharply, keep K=128 as the mainline anchor and stabilize family-specific internals rather than abandoning the architecture.
If K=64 is quality-competitive but runtime is poor, follow with a performance-focused implementation pass instead of treating it as an architectural miss.
